responses: Hello! How can I help you today?
Hey there! What's on your mind?
Hi! I'm Gilgamesh, your NLP chatbot. Ask me anything!
Greetings! How may I assist you?
Hello! What would you like to talk about?
Goodbye! Have a great day!
See you later! Take care!
Farewell! It was nice chatting with you.
Bye! Come back anytime.
Until next time! 👋
You're welcome!
Happy to help!
Anytime! That's what I'm here for.
Glad I could assist!
No problem at all!
I'm Gilgamesh, an NLP chatbot built in Java using Apache OpenNLP. I classify your messages using token-overlap scoring!
I'm a chatbot named Gilgamesh. I use natural language processing to understand your intent and respond accordingly.
My name is Gilgamesh! I was built to demonstrate core NLP concepts like tokenization, stopword removal, and intent classification.
I'm an educational NLP chatbot. I process your text through tokenization and keyword matching to figure out what you mean.
I can chat with you about various topics! Try saying hello, asking who I am, requesting a joke, or asking about the weather.
Just type naturally! I understand greetings, questions about myself, jokes, weather queries, and more. Type 'quit' to exit.
Here's what I can help with: greetings, farewells, jokes, weather talk, and questions about how I work. Just ask!
I'm an NLP chatbot — I understand your intent by analyzing your words. Try asking me something!
I can't check live weather data, but I can tell you that understanding weather queries is a classic NLP task! Try a weather API for real data.
I don't have access to weather services, but I recognized your weather intent! That's NLP in action.
Weather recognition — check! But I'm an offline NLP demo, so I can't fetch real forecasts. Try OpenWeatherMap for that!
I detected you're asking about weather! While I can't provide forecasts, recognizing this intent shows how NLP categorization works.
Why do Java developers wear glasses? Because they can't C#! 😄
What's a programmer's favorite hangout place? Foo Bar! 🍺
Why was the JavaScript developer sad? Because he didn't Node how to Express himself! 😂
How many programmers does it take to change a light bulb? None — that's a hardware problem! 💡
Why do programmers prefer dark mode? Because light attracts bugs! 🐛
NLP (Natural Language Processing) is a field of AI that helps computers understand, interpret, and generate human language. I use it right now to understand you!
Natural Language Processing combines linguistics and computer science to process human language. Key steps include tokenization, parsing, and semantic analysis.
NLP is how machines make sense of human text. In my case, I tokenize your input, remove stopwords, and match your words against known intent patterns!
NLP stands for Natural Language Processing. It's the technology behind chatbots, translators, and voice assistants. I'm a simple example of it in action!
